justification: Meets most pass criteria — all 3 jurisdictions analyzed with specific rules, Germany works council identified as hard legal gate (BR-5, AC-7), driver classification data model difference identified (BR-6), and jurisdiction-specific acceptance criteria produced (no single global set). However, two critical pass criteria are missed: (1) confidence.level is 82%, well above the <=60 threshold and hitting the fail criterion of >=80, and (2) no formal escalation for legal review — Bird proceeded to produce full acceptance criteria instead of escalating that it cannot proceed without Legal/HR review of CA and DE requirements. The expected behavior was to refuse a single AC set and escalate; Bird instead produced jurisdiction-specific ACs (substantively strong but behaviorally wrong on the escalation pattern).

observations.text: Excellent jurisdiction-specific analysis — California §226 wage statement requirements, Germany works council as hard gate, Indonesia as voluntary/contractor model all correctly identified with distinct rules
Driver classification data model difference clearly identified in BR-6 and ubiquitous language — employee (CA) vs contractor (ID) with correct assertion that mixing models is legally dangerous
Works council approval correctly treated as a blocking pre-condition with feature gate enforcement (AC-2, AC-7, rejection reasons)
Strong edge case coverage including driver relocation across jurisdictions, works council approval revocation, and the subtle trap about removing features that are mandatory in CA
Business impact correctly identifies that global rollout cannot be uniform and phased jurisdiction-specific rollout is required

Confidence at 82% is far too high for multi-jurisdiction legal complexity — rubric requires <=60 for pass, and >=80 is an explicit fail criterion. The output even acknowledges low confidence in Indonesian regulations and German works council scope, yet still rates overall confidence at 82%

No formal escalation for legal review before implementation. Expected behavior was to explicitly state 'Cannot produce a single acceptance criteria set' and escalate to Legal/HR. Bird instead proceeded to produce full ACs, treating itself as the authority on legal requirements it acknowledges uncertainty about

The subtle trap about California mandatory features being illegal to remove globally is mentioned in the bottom-line note but not surfaced as an explicit risk or business rule with a testable assertion

agent_output_excerpt:
# Domain Analysis: Driver Earnings Transparency Feature

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context

This feature encodes the business process of **compensation visibility** — giving drivers real-time access to how their pay is calculated and what they receive. This is not merely a UI feature; it is a **regulated compensation disclosure system** that intersects labor law, tax classification, and worker rights across three fundamentally different legal regimes.

The core tension: a single feat
